Q5 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Astronomer: Most stars are born in groups of thousands, each star in a group forming from the same parent cloud of gas. Each cloud has a unique homogeneous chemical composition. Therefore whenever two stars have the same chemical composition as each other, they must have originated from the same cloud of gas.
Which of the following, if true, would most strengthen the astronomer’s argument?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
It is given that each cloud has a unique homogeneous chemical composition and the stars originated from the same cloud of gas have the same chemical composition. Thus, it can be concluded that whenever a star forms, it inherits the chemical composition of its parent cloud of gas. Hence, option (c) is correct.

Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

People tend to estimate the likelihood of an event’s occurrence according to its salience; that is, according to how strongly and how often it comes to their attention. By placement and headlines, newspapers emphasize stories about local crime over stories about crime elsewhere and about many other major events.
It can be concluded on the basis of the statements above that, if they are true, which of the following is most probably also true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
We know that newspapers’ coverage of local crimes is stronger than that of non-local crimes.
Nothing is said about frequency of coverage so, we can not assume anything about it. However, all else being equal, we can assume that people tend to think the local crimes outnumber non-local crimes.

P : Amount of simple interest accrued after 4 years.
Q : Rate of interest per annum.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
To find the sum, difference between C.I. and S.I., the time and the rate of interest are needed.
∴ Only Q is necessary.

Q21 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements about the User Datagram Protocol (UDP) is TRUE?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• UDP is a connectionless, unreliable transport-layer protocol that does not guarantee delivery, order, or retransmission.
• It uses a 16-bit checksum to detect data corruption.
• The checksum calculation includes a pseudo-header (from the IP header: source IP, destination
IP, protocol, and UDP length), but the checksum itself does not protect the IP header.

Q28 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the combinational circuit shown below:

 
 
If output F = 1, the possible input combinations (I3 I2 I1 I0) is/are
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Since, given output of EX-OR gate, F = 1 and one of the input of gate is 1, hence the other input must be zero (0).
i.e., output of 4 × 1 multiplexer is ‘0’.
Since select lines S1 S0 = 01, hence I1 is propagated to the output. Thus, I1 = 0 in the given possible
combinations. Hence, option (a) is correct.

Q30 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the simple graph G given in figure given below:

 
 
Then, which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?
Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
1. Recall that the diameter is the maximum of all shortest path lengths between pairs of vertices.
Note that the shortest path length between D and F is 3, and all other pairs of non-adjacent
vertices share a neighbor.
2. One possible solution is (A, F, E, C, D, B, A). This cycle and its reverse should constitute all
possible solutions.
3. One possible 3-coloring is: {A, D, E} red; {B, F} green; C blue. Because there exists an oddlength
cycle (e.g. (B, D, C)), no 2-coloring exists. Therefore the given coloring uses the least
possible number of colors.
4. True. This follows from the fact that there exist vertices with odd degree; e.g. B.

Q45 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

During a TCP session setup, Host A advertizes a window scaling factor of 7, and Host B advertizes a window scaling factor of 5. Later in the session, Host A sends data and advertizes a TCP window value of 20,000 bytes in its TCP header. Host B acknowledges and advertizes a TCP
window value of 40,000 bytes. Assuming no congestion or packet loss, determine the actual maximum amount of unacknowledged data that each host can transmit at a time.

Correct answer: B, A
Solution

(b)
(a) Host A → 200000 × 27 = 2560000 bytes
(b) Host B → 40000 × 25 = 1280000 bytes
Therefore, Host A → 2560000 bytes
Host B → 1280000 bytes

Q48 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A directed graph G(V, E) with |V| = 6 and |E| = 9 contains a few negative-weight edges but no negative-weight cycles.
The graph is represented using an adjacency list (V1 → V2, wt). A variant of the Bellman-Ford algorithm is run from a source vertex S = 0.
After 5 full iterations of edge relaxations, the distance array dist[ ] contains final shortest path distances from S to all other nodes.
Suppose the edge list is as follows:
(0 → 1, 4), (0 → 2, 5),
(1 → 2, –3), (2 → 3, 2),
(3 → 1, 1), (1 → 4, 6),
(2 → 5, 4), (5 → 3, –2),
(4 → 5, –1)
Based on this, which of the following statements are necessarily TRUE?

Correct answer: C, D
Solution

(c, d)
• Bellman-Ford guarantees convergence in |V| – 1 = 5 iterations only if no shorter paths exist.
If no update is made in the final iteration, the algorithm halts early. So, update is not necessarily
made in the 5th.
• A cycle with total weight 0 does not cause infinite updates. Only negative-weight cycles do.
Hence, this does not make Bellman-Ford loop indefinitely.
• Bellman-Ford always converges in at most |V| – 1 = 5 iterations if there is no negative cycle,
which is stated in the question.
• Adding an edge (3 → 0, –10) creates a cycle: 0 → 1 → 2 → 3 → 0
Total weight: 4 – 3 + 2 – 10 = –7 ⇒ a negative-weight cycle, and Bellman-Ford will detect it in
the 6th iteration.

Q53 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the code given below:

 
Which of the following is TRUE?
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Initially, I is equal to zero. I become 5 since case 0 is true, and 16 because there is no break
statement until the last statement of the switch block. Because of i++, I becomes 17 before the next iteration begins.
Because no case is true in the next iteration, execution returns to default and I becomes 21.
In C, if one of the cases is true, the switch block is processed until it reaches the break statement.
If there is no break statement, all cases are processed after the true case.

Q60 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the below are undecidable?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• (a) is undecidable as this is non-trivial question on RE languages.
• (b) is ambiguous problem, well known undecidable.
• (c) is membership problem in CFG which is decidable.
• (d) is undecidable

HAVING COMMISSION > 200;

‘IT’;
(iii) SELECT * FROM EMP WHERE SALARY BETWEEN 6000 AND 8000 GROUP BY DEPT ORDER
BY EMPID;

> 6000;
Correct answer: B
Solution

Q65 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following grammar and semantic rules: A → X Y Z
X.val = f(A.in)
Y.val = g(X.val)
Z.val = h(Y.val)
A.out = k(Z.val) and A → M N
M.in = N.out Which of the following statements is TRUE?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• In the first production, each attribute depends only on the parent or left siblings — this is
L-attributed.
• In the second, M.in = N.out depends on the right sibling (N), violating L-attributed definition.
